issue_title: ISession interface not respected for wamp.protocol.ApplicationSession.join
issue_body:
It seems there is a slight difference between in the interface `ISession.leave` and it implementation in the `protocol.ApplicationSession.leave`:

https://github.com/crossbario/autobahn-python/blob/master/autobahn/wamp/interfaces.py#L298
```python
class ApplicationSession(BaseSession):
    ...
    @abc.abstractmethod
    def leave(self, reason=None, message=None):
        ...
```

https://github.com/crossbario/autobahn-python/blob/master/autobahn/wamp/protocol.py#L1040
```python
class ISession(object):
    ...
    def leave(self, reason=None, log_message=None):
        """
        Implements :func:`autobahn.wamp.interfaces.ISession.leave`
        """
       ...
```

This should cause a crash if  `ApplicationSession.leave` is called with it params as kwargs  (`log_message` vs `message`)

And just for curiosity: why `ApplicationSession` doesn't inherit from `ISession` given it should implement it ?

issue_title: With Ubuntu 17.04 and systemd, --stop of a JVM process kills my desktop session
issue_body:
Since upgrading to Ubuntu 17.04, an `sm --stop` of a JVM process kills my desktop session.

This appears to because where as previously, the ppid was a Session Init /sbin/upstart, now it is a user mode /bin/systemd .

Presumably, sending a SIGINT to upstart was benign, sending it to systemd is catastrophic - it treats it as SIGTERM.

As a workaround, https://github.com/lutnos/service-manager/commit/aa44f6077121a10692dba885c84b1a8efeecfd70, I am no longer killing the ppid, but I am not at all clear in which environments this is or was necessary.

ATB

issue_title: Figure out what's going on with port/vport in 2.7.2
issue_body:
Long story...

Last December, working on a10-openstack, we discovered that the "port" parameter for virtual servers was actually "vport".  We changed it and stuff appeared to work.

Over the last 11 months, we have gone back and forth fixing this bug and re-introducing it.  I think we need to create a generic library for 2.7.2/4.0 discrepancies that we can use across all projects so we can stop the see-saw that is this bug.

issue_title: Document how to pass args to get
issue_body:
The iLO5 REST API Docs say that iLO5 supports the OData $expand syntax (https://hewlettpackard.github.io/ilo-rest-api-docs/ilo5/#odata-expand-syntax).

Could you write an example or somehow document how to pass args to get, such as:

response = REDFISH_OBJ.get('/redfish/v1/Systems/1/SmartStorage/ArrayControllers/0/DiskDrives/', args={'$expand':'.'})

It took me a while to figure this out, and having an example might help folks.

This seems like a good idea if you're using iLO5 and have a lot of items of the same type (e.g. a DL380 populated with 24 drives) rather than having to get the Members list and then do a get on each individual one.

issue_title: Change if/else if/else to switch in deisctl
issue_body: There are multiple places in `deisctl` where we use `if/else if/else` trees rather than `switch` statements. The Go idiom (http://golang.org/doc/effective_go.html#switch) is to prefer switch over if/else trees.

issue_title: Error when roles or permission list contains an entry that has a non-existent identity
issue_body:
`globus endpoint role list <uuid>` and `globus endpoint permission list <uuid>` assume that every entry in the list will resolve to an identity so the `username` can be printed. However, Transfer allows ACLs and roles to be assigned to arbitrary UUIDs.

Example:
![screen shot 2017-06-19 at 9 31 05 am](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/214142/27290162-0212f46e-54d2-11e7-831e-2a6d809ff7ca.png)

CLI output:
```
globus endpoint role list 78ff1934-4638-11e7-bd4f-22000b9a448b
IndexError: list index out of range
```

Will fix as part of #327.

issue_title: Support to auto balance SP to create NAS server
issue_body:
The current implementation is that: if SP is not passed in (is None) to create NAS server, storops will use the first item of SP list.

This feature is to auto balance the SP of creating NAS server when SP is not passed in.
SPB will be chosen only when the existing NAS servers on SPA are more than the ones on SPB. Otherwise, SPA will be chosen.

issue_title: Better error message if dependencies are incompatible
issue_body:
_From @bilderbuchi on November 24, 2015 10:9_

Recently, I tried to install pylint (#543), and I got this error message:

```
Z:\>conda install pylint
Fetching package metadata: ....
Solving package specifications: ............
Error: Unsatisfiable package specifications.
Generating hint:
[      COMPLETE      ]|##################################################| 100%


Hint: the following packages conflict with each other:
  - pylint
  - python 3.5*

Use 'conda info pylint' etc. to see the dependencies for each package.

Note that the following features are enabled:
  - vc14
```

Pylint conflicts with python 3.5? Interesting, because running `conda info pylint` as the error message suggested yields (among a load of others) one section involving py35:

```
pylint 1.4.2 py35_0
-------------------
file name   : pylint-1.4.2-py35_0.tar.bz2
name        : pylint
version     : 1.4.2
build number: 0
build string: py35_0
channel     : defaults
size        : 246 KB
date        : 2015-10-31
license     : GPL
license_family: GPL2
md5         : 39b28ee413f6960a1dc1d89c12e48638
installed environments:
dependencies:
    astroid 1.3.4
    python 3.5*
```

so, it seems pylint conflicts with its own dependency (while at the same time offering a version for py35)?!?

Some helpful hints reveal that it's a _dependency_  of pylint that fails here (in this case: logilab-common did not have a py35 version out yet), but the error message does not in any way imply that that could be the cause - the user has to manually climb down the dependency tree to find out (easy with pylint, as it was only 2 steps, but this could be pretty involved, especially with a branching dependency tree). 

It would be great if the error message would be more expressive about the fact that a dependency is incompatible, not the package-to-install itself.


_Copied from original issue: ContinuumIO/anaconda-issues#556_

issue_title: Feature request, flags to prevent up/downgrading packages on incremental installations
issue_body:
We (Numba devs) keep getting hit by the following pattern in the giant number of configurations Numba is tested on. Essentially, incrementally building up conda envs can lead to unexpected downgrades etc which are not desirable in a testing environment (we test with `conda build` too, but this checks the path of users installing stuff manually).

As a motivating example, when testing Python 3.7, we set up envs based on the test channel for Python 3.7, then installed e.g. SciPy for testing, but as there was no SciPy+Python 3.7 build at the time, Python itself got downgraded to 3.6 so SciPy would install. As a result we were then testing on something we didn't expect to be and it e.g. erroneously passed.

One suggestion that might help with this is to add a flag to be able to do e.g. `conda install foo --downgrade-is-error` to install `foo` into an existing env, and if as a result of installing `foo` things that already exist in the env need downgrading, that's an error. The inverse flag `--upgrade-is-error` does the same but errors if installing `foo` would cause upgrades. These flags also should be able to be used simultaneously.

Thanks for considering this.

issue_title: conda should install python3 automatically if needed
issue_body:
The tool hivtrace is a py3k only tool (bioconda channel). If we are in a python2 env, let's say miniconda2 `conda install hivtrace` will fail because conda is trying to get a package for python2. This results in a version conflict.
The correct way to install this package is `conda install hivtrace python==3.5.1` to tell conda that you actually want to have python3. I think this should be done automatically. If there is no python2 version, python3 should be a dependency and it should not fail at first hand.

issue_title: BlockingIOError(11, 'write could not complete without blocking', 4048) on Travis
issue_body:
Hi Conda team,

We've been facing issues on Travis machines, and it's been hard to solve them. Currently we have `conda install` commands that are failing on those machines with the error in title (more logging below). The command works fine locally. The failures do not occur always at the same package. I tried googl'ing the error and conda or travis, but I couldn't find any information. I also saw this ticket (https://github.com/conda/conda/issues/6473), so maybe there is something general going in Travis machines regarding IO. Is there anything that could help track down this problem? Thank you!

issue_title: change the message returned from command error
issue_body:
Sounds great! Perhaps even go one step further and include node.connection.error.command_error in the stringification of CommandError? I think most users won't remember to look at this field, and it usually holds the interesting data. Perhaps we don't want to include it in the stringification of certain error message, but if error.code == 1000 (general error), then it is definitely more useful to get the actual message.

continuation of issue #4

I am using Curator version 4.1.0. I have the action file like below;

```
actions:
  1:
    action: snapshot
    description: >-
      Archiving indices older then 10 days

issue_title: IndexList _get_metadata consumes excessive amount of memory
issue_body:
I've got an ES cluster maintaining 32 days of LogStash data in open indexes, and another 60 in closed indexes. I'm using the standard LogStash index naming scheme.

My issue is that running a Delete action can cause Python to consume several gigs of memory, presumably because it's pulling down and storing in memory metadata for all of the indexes, which is (due to the sheer number of mappings in each index) is quite large - about 110MB of JSON, without pretty-printing enabled.

Here's what I see if I profile `IndexList._get_metadata` with memory_profiler:

```
Filename: /home/ec2-user/ansible-env/lib/python2.7/site-packages/curator/indexlist.py

Line #    Mem usage    Increment   Line Contents
================================================
   143     34.4 MiB      0.0 MiB       @profile
   144                                 def _get_metadata(self):
   145                                     """
   146                                     Populate `index_info` with index `size_in_bytes` and doc count
   147                                     information for each index.
   148                                     """
   149     34.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           self.loggit.debug('Getting index metadata')
   150     34.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           self.empty_list_check()
   151     34.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           index_lists = chunk_index_list(self.indices)
   152   1463.6 MiB   1429.3 MiB           for l in index_lists:
   153                                         working_list = (
   154     34.4 MiB  -1429.3 MiB                   self.client.cluster.state(
   155   1463.6 MiB   1429.3 MiB                       index=to_csv(l),metric='metadata'
   156   1463.6 MiB      0.0 MiB                   )['metadata']['indices']
   157                                         )
   158   1463.6 MiB      0.0 MiB               if working_list:
   159   1463.6 MiB      0.0 MiB                   for index in list(working_list.keys()):
   160   1463.6 MiB      0.0 MiB                       s = self.index_info[index]
   161   1463.6 MiB      0.0 MiB                       wl = working_list[index]

   163                                                     self.loggit.warn(
   164                                                         'Index: {0} has no "creation_date"! This implies '
   165                                                         'that the index predates Elasticsearch v1.4. For '
   166                                                         'safety, this index will be removed from the '
   167                                                         'actionable list.'.format(index)
   168                                                     )
   169                                                     self.__not_actionable(index)

```

An easy fix for this is to reduce the chunk size returned by `chunk_index_list` - if I reduce the max length from 3072 to 64 characters, it takes a few more round-trips to collect the data, but memory never grows out of control:

issue_title: AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT and AZURE_STORAGE_KEY handling is inconsistent
issue_body:
The help for the `az storage` commands tells users that they can set `AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT` and `AZURE_STORAGE_KEY`, so that they don't have to enter them repeatedly. These are handled differently, leading to a confusing user experience.

`AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT` **must** be set using 'export'
```shell
# does not work
AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT=foo

# works
export AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT=foo
```

`AZURE_STORAGE_KEY` can be specified as either an environment var or a bash var
```shell
# works
AZURE_STORAGE_KEY=bar

# also works
export AZURE_STORAGE_KEY=bar
```

I've hit this repeatedly, thinking `AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT` handling was just broken, until I tried adding 'export' and it worked.  This issue is to request that they be handled the same - and that users be able to specify them via environment vars or bash variables, because it's easy to forget 'export' while writing a script.


---

issue_title: az monitor metrics list should support aggregation type argument
issue_body:
The azure metrics API supports multiple aggregation types e.g. average, total, minimum, maximum, etc (for more info see [here](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/monitoring-and-diagnostics/monitoring-rest-api-walkthrough#retrieve-metric-values)). This is passed as an additional argument in the odata query string e.g. `(aggregationType eq 'Total')`.

The `az monitor metrics list` command does not accept this argument, which means you'll only get the default aggregation. This can block scenarios such as trying to find out the total number of Azure Functions executions you have via the CLI.

Environment info:
```
azure-cli (2.0.16)
monitor (0.0.9)
```

issue_title: No option to specify template when creating a deployment
issue_body:
When creating a new deployment with `az resource group deployment create`, there is no option to specify a template URI. It appears that only a local json file can be specified. There is also then no interactive prompts when passing in .parameters file (which also must be local). This is probably fine if the user is creating their own templates.

The Azure quickstart templates (https://github.com/Azure/azure-quickstart-templates), however, can't be used unless you curl the json files from Github and then manually edit the .parameters file accordingly. 

Trying to use template in Github repo rather than local file:

```
ifoulds@Ubuntu:~$ az resource group deployment create -n mongodb -g mongodb --template-file-path https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/azure-quickstart-templates/master/mongodb-on-ubuntu/azuredeploy.json
File 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/azure-quickstart-templates/master/mongodb-on-ubuntu/azuredeploy.json' contains error: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/azure-quickstart-templates/master/mongodb-on-ubuntu/azuredeploy.json'

Once curl'd the json files and edited accordingly, the following works:

ifoulds@Ubuntu:~$ az resource group deployment create -n mongodb -g mongodb --template-file-path azuredeploy.json --parameters-file-path azuredeploy.parameters.json

issue_title: Wrong cookies format when sending request
issue_body:
While sending cookie with special symbols or spaces, aiohttp adds unnecessary quotes around cookie value.

This only occurs when cookies are added to session manually. `ClientSession(cookies={..})` or when updating existing session.

- Example what cookie should look like:
```
User-Agent: User-Agent
Accept: */*
Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate
Host: httpbin.org
Cookie: cookie_name=cookie_value
```

- Example when unnecessary quotes are added:
```
User-Agent: User-Agent
Accept: */*
Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate
Host: httpbin.org
Cookie: cookie_name="cookie_ value"
```

This creates problem for server side to read cookies correctly.

issue_title: Proposal: merge server and web github labels
issue_body:
Historically *web* for highlevel `aiohttp.web` and *server* for lowlevel aiohttp server `aiohttp.web.Server`.
I see very low activity for low-level server in our issues, let's just drop *web* label.
We'll have *client* for `aiohttp.ClientSession` related issues, *server* for server ones.
Sure, there are many issues reflected in both parts like HTTP parser, multiparts, payload writers etc. -- but their context is understandable usually from title and initial message.
Let's not add a new label -- it will confuse newbies and don't help me personally.

In master we have introduced some new blue colors for the marked cell indicator and `In` prompt. Looks a bit funny against the bold `darkred` of the `Out` prompt. @cameronoelsen can you do some prototypes of a color scheme for the cell that:

* Has slightly more contrast between the marked cell and `In` blue
* Has a red for the `Out` that matches those better
* The `In` and `Out` are visually similar (saturation and brightness).

issue_title: cannot "download as IPython notebook" a read-only notebook
issue_body:
When I open a read-only ipython notebook I cannot download it as an ipynb file, even if it hasn't been modified.

Steps to reproduce:
  1. make some existing `*.ipynb` file readonly on your file system
  2. open it with jupyter notebook (the editor will flash "auto-save disabled" and show a small logo "this notebook is read-only")
  3. select   File > Download as > IPython notebook (*.ipynb) (this just opens a blank page, does not download anything, and shows no error on the console)


Context: I'm trying to setup a public Jupyter installation where users can test some of my tools.  In this installation I have some example notebooks that I have marked as read-only because I don't want them to be modified by mistake, but I'd still like them to be downloadable.

issue_title: Discovery importing dependent modules multiple times
issue_body:
If I have reactive/a.py and reactive/b.py that both import lib/shared.py, shared.py ends up being imported twice times and any side effects run twice. Global state will be reset, potentially discarding modifications made by a.py or b.py. atstart/atexit handlers will be registered multiple times.

Work around is to import the modules from the hooks/ stub.

issue_title: Better tooling needed for debugging filestore
issue_body: The `RawHandler` helps but this is still very awkward to do.

Summarizing a conversation with @stuwilkins:

Fortunately, we have independently reached the same conclusion: `get_images` is awkward. It takes the return value from an asset [née filestore] Handler and, whatever it is, wraps it in a PIMS object. Oftentimes, the handler itself has returned a PIMS object, resulting in a PIMS object inside anoter PIMS object. This does not behave well; for example, it doesn't work with `pims.pipeline`. Or, perhaps `h5py.Dataset`, resulting in two different types of lazy objects, nested. It's confusing.

At both CSX and CHX, I have anecdotally found this awkward and confusing. It seems like @stuwilkins, @tacaswell, and I have consensus there.

We surely shouldn't _break_ `get_images` in this release, and we maybe should support it forever to avoid breaking old notebook, but we should no longer recommend it.

The original motivation was to save users from this have to navigate the structure of an Event document just to get their images:

```python
events = db.get_events(header, fields=['fccd_image'])
imgs = (ev['data']['fccd_image'] for ev in events)
```

This is, of course, still an issue, but we could provide an alternative that defers more power to the Handler to determine what the useful return type is (rather than shoehorning everything into PIMS).

```python
imgs = header.data('fccd_image')
```
supported by a new method on Header:

```python
class Header:
    def data(field, stream_name='primary', fill=False):
        for ev in self.events(stream_name=stream_name, fields=[field], fill=fill):
            yield ev['data'][field]
    ...
```

issue_title: s3am block size is too small in alignment pipeline
issue_body:
Specifically, it isn't set, and thus s3am defaults to a 5MB block size. Since a file is limited to 10k blocks, this limits file size to ~50GB.

We should do something like https://github.com/BD2KGenomics/toil-scripts/blob/master/src/toil_scripts/rnaseq_cgl/rnaseq_cgl_pipeline.py#L873 and up the block size to 64M. We should also set the upload parallelism.

issue_title: [Bug Report] appearance of grass in wolf-sheep model
issue_body:
Small issue, but I've noticed, when running the wolf-sheep example with grass enabled, that the grass appears black and obscures the sheep. I believe I have fixed this in the corresponding server script.

I made the changes in my fork, but reading the contrib guidelines I've opened this issue first. I presume I should also create a new branch before making the pull request? I am sorry: I am still learning GitHub etiquette. Many thanks for your work.

this is to help pytest-dev/pytest#1372

the idea is, that if a hook is invoked with a missing, but specced argument, then the default is used instead

so if a hook caller would call `hook.pytest_deselected(items=...)'
and the hook was defined with`def pytest_deselected(items, reason='No Reason Given)`
then the receivers would receive the defined default reason

in order to support deprecation of such usage we could later devise a mark for deprecated defaults

I was running into an issue with pluggy, because [it eats `DistributionNotFound` errors]((https://github.com/pytest-dev/pluggy/blob/master/pluggy/manager.py#L266-L267)). In my case, this prevented most of my installed pytest plugins from being used, without any indication as to why.

The root cause of the `DistributionNotFound` error was a missing dependency. I installed pytest from a [Pipfile.lock] that was (presumably) generated on a Unix system, where pytest does not depend on the `colorama` package. However, I was running on a Windows system, so pytest was installed without all of its dependencies. When pluggy tries to load pytest plugins, which (I presume) depend again on pytest, `pkg_resources` notices this missing dependency and raises a `DistributionNotFound` error, which is silently eaten by pluggy.

For reference, here is the traceback that's being eaten (by adding a `traceback.print_exc()` inside pluggy):

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "c:\users\brenda\.virtualenvs\django-machina-_lipc3zt\lib\site-packages\pluggy\manager.py", line 254, in load_setuptools_entrypoints
    plugin = ep.load()
  File "c:\users\brenda\.virtualenvs\django-machina-_lipc3zt\lib\site-packages\pkg_resources\__init__.py", line 2320, in load
    self.require(*args, **kwargs)
  File "c:\users\brenda\.virtualenvs\django-machina-_lipc3zt\lib\site-packages\pkg_resources\__init__.py", line 2343, in require
    items = working_set.resolve(reqs, env, installer, extras=self.extras)
  File "c:\users\brenda\.virtualenvs\django-machina-_lipc3zt\lib\site-packages\pkg_resources\__init__.py", line 777, in resolve
    raise DistributionNotFound(req, requirers)
pkg_resources.DistributionNotFound: The 'colorama; sys_platform == "win32"' distribution was not found and is required by pytest
```

Why does pluggy eat this error? I traced the history back to the pytest repo and the change was introduced in this commit: pytest-dev/pytest@1bc444d5c8177cb7e1f2f8c14d8cd232aac54201, but that does not really tell me why it was introduced....

I think this is just an awareness thing.  But based on the licensing pytest-html is not longer actually being distributed via MPL and is officially GPL3 due to the addition of ansi2html.  Since this is a tool, I'm not sure it really matters, but it would be good to have that identified somewhere.

issue_title: --firefox-path gives permission denied error on osx Sierra
issue_body:
I need to be able to run two versions of firefox to be able to test with both legacy firefox driver and geckodriver.

The versions I'm using are 47.0.2 and latest (as of writing this that is 52.0)

Here's what I've done:

- Installed 47.0.2 via dmg from mozilla.
- Renamed the .app to `Firefox47`
- Installed 52.0 via brew (`brew install Caskroom/cask/firefox`)
- Renamed that .app to `Firefox52`
- Renamed `Firefox47` back to `Firefox`

Running tests with pytest setting `marionette`capa to `false` works fine (I don't have to specify `--firefox-path`)

Running tests with pytest setting `marionette`capa to `true` and setting `--firefox-path` to `/Applications/Firefox52.app/Contents/MacOS/` does not work and errors out with: `WebDriverException: Message: Failed to start browser /Applications/Firefox52.app/Contents/MacOS/: permission denied`

We're running very tight firewalls on our machines and would appreciate if the following error message would state what IPs to allow traffic from:

```
IMPORTANT NOTES:
 - The following 'connection' errors were reported by the server:

   Domains: domain.tld

   To fix these errors, please make sure that your domain name was

Thanks for your consideration

issue_title: Issue when parsing ISO8601 date string on GAE
issue_body:
I'm using version 2.4.1 in both a local GAE environment as well as in a local ipython instance. 
In both I attempt to parse the string using:
```
asdf = dateutil.parser.parse(u'2014-06-01T00:00:00.000Z')
print asdf.tzinfo
```
On the local machine, this properly shows tzutc as the result
When using the GAE environment, this instead returns a datetime with tzlocal

It looks like this is being set in parser.py at line 424 (425 after my pdb insertion)
```
(Pdb) l
420  	                    raise ValueError("Offset must be tzinfo subclass, "
421  	                                     "tz string, or int offset.")
422  	                ret = ret.replace(tzinfo=tzinfo)
423  	            elif res.tzname and res.tzname in time.tzname:
424  	                import pdb; pdb.set_trace()
425  ->	                ret = ret.replace(tzinfo=tz.tzlocal())
```

I'm not sure what the issue is but this seems very strange.

issue_title: best pratice: impact of timezone shifts to data
issue_body:
Fellow pvlib users,
I would like to ask how you handle timezone (TZ) shifts of data supplied by the provider in UTC.
When the `tz_convert` is applied, one ends up with incomplete days at beginning and end of the series.

Let's say I have a timeseries for the whole of 2014 in hourly data.

```
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

days =365 * 24 * 60

rng = pd.date_range('1/1/2014', periods=days, freq='T')
ts = pd.Series(np.random.randint(low=200, high=1000, size=len(rng)), index=rng)

tz_dataset = 'UTC'
tz_target = 'Atlantic/Bermuda'

ts_utc = ts.tz_localize(tz_dataset)

ts_utc.index.summary()
Out[345]: u'DatetimeIndex: 525600 entries, 2014-01-01 00:00:00+00:00 to 2014-12-31 23:59:00+00:00\nFreq: T'
```

Then I convert it to local
```
ts_tz = ts_utc.tz_convert(tz_target)
ts_tz = ts_tz.asfreq(ts_tz.index.inferred_freq)

```
naturally, the values get moved into the previous day:
```
ts_tz.index.summary()
Out[344]: u'DatetimeIndex: 525600 entries, 2013-12-31 20:00:00-04:00 to 2014-12-31 19:59:00-04:00\nFreq: T'
```
This is then significant when one wants to have complete days, retrieve extact time matching the measured data or aggregate to monthly data.

Any hints or help is appreciated. Thanks in advance.

Note:
Although being more a pandas issue, I think this is more prelevant to climate / solar data in this context.

issue_title: Command line argument parsing breaks when the path to the script contains spaces.
issue_body:
This one had be scratching my head for a while trying to figure out why my experiments wouldn't run on my new computer.  At first, I thought it was because my computer had downloaded a newer version of the API, but I eventually figured out it because there was spaces in my project's path.

I'm not sure how to provide a reproducible example, since its dependent on WHERE you put the files, rather than the code itself.  But, I think this will illustrate the problem:

When I run my experiment, the usage description sacred generates looks like this:

```
Usage:
  /Users/kpyancey/Projects/Korean Elaborator/Library/experiments/evaluate_wsd.py [(with UPDATE...)] [options]
  /Users/kpyancey/Projects/Korean Elaborator/Library/experiments/evaluate_wsd.py help [COMMAND]
  /Users/kpyancey/Projects/Korean Elaborator/Library/experiments/evaluate_wsd.py (-h | --help)
  /Users/kpyancey/Projects/Korean Elaborator/Library/experiments/evaluate_wsd.py COMMAND [(with UPDATE...)] [options]
```

What goes wrong is that because of the space between Korean and Elaborator, docopt interprets "Elaborator/Library/experiments/evaluate_wsd.py" in the usage text as being a required named argument.  So, the script wont run unless you provide "Elaborator/Library/experiments/evaluate_wsd.py" as an argument (or change the directory path to one without spaces).

Simple fix is probably just modify sacred to put quotes around the script path if it contains spaces, or to use only the filename instead of the full path.

It is very convenient to 
```
./run.py save_config with x=123
./run.py with config.json
```

but this does not work with ingredients
```
./run.py save_config with x=123 data.y=123
./run.py with config.json
```

(data is an ingredient)
resulting in
`KeyError: 'Added a new config entry "data.y" that is not used anywhere'`

This is probably because you cannot override ingredient config values in the main experiment config (or at least I don't know how to do it).

I'd like to have `Conv1DLayer`, `Conv2DLayer` and `Conv3DLayer` available. Unfortunately Theano's support for 3D convolutions is a bit of a mess imho, there are some different implementations (see [documentation](http://deeplearning.net/software/theano/library/tensor/nnet/conv.html) for details):
- `theano.tensor.nnet.Conv3D.conv3D`, which I guess is the default. Its expected input shape configuration is `b01tc` (b = batch, 0 = width, 1 = height, t = time, c = channels, note that 0, 1 and t are basically interchangeable), which is sort of unusual because the channel dimension is last. In practice this is apparently CPU only because the GPU implementation is slow (the docs even encourage using `conv3d2d` on GPU instead).
- `theano.tensor.nnet.conv3d2d.conv3d` has `btc01` as its input shape configuration. Also kind of weird to have the channel dimension in the middle there. This works by turning the 3D convolution into a 2D convolution, and given the recent improvements for the 2D convolution, this is probably the most useful implementation.
- `theano.sandbox.cuda.fftconv.conv3d_fft` has `bct01` as its input shape configuration, which is the most natural way to do things imho (since `conv2d` uses `bc01`). This is a 3D version of `conv2d_fft`. I don't know how practical this is because FFT convolutions use a lot of memory. There is no gradient implemented
- There is an optimization that swaps `theano.tensor.nnet.Conv3D.conv3D` for `theano.sandbox.cuda.fftconv.conv3d_fft`. It must be enabled manually.

Since `conv3d_fft` has no gradient implemented, there is no point in supporting it directly. Instead we should support `Conv3D.conv3D`.

I think we should also support `conv3d2d.conv3d`. Afaik there is no optimization to replace  `Conv3D.conv3D` by `conv3d2d.conv3d`. So that means the `Conv3DLayer` class needs a switch to choose the implementation. This can just be an `implementation` keyword argument on the constructor (which is how I've tackled this issue in the past in layers.py).

In general I think having an `implementation` keyword for all of the `Conv*DLayers` is a good idea, there are a lot of different ways to do things and although there are sometimes optimizations that can be enabled manually, those aren't always available. And for the 1D convolution there is currently no implementation at all (but I have a bunch of code for that already).

What should be the default implementation to use? I think having `conv3d2d.conv3d` as the default makes the most sense since it is probably the most practically usable implementation these days. But we should definitely support `Conv3D.conv3D` as well.

Regardless of this, I think `Conv3DLayer` should use `bct01` input order by default. This means some reshapes may be necessary. We could also add a keyword argument to disable this reshaping, as a performance optimization. In that case the user would be expected to ensure that the input to the layer already has the right order.

Hi,

Are there any plans to add optional restrictions to the TransformerLayer?
e.g., do only translation and scaling in one axis, etc.

issue_title: Rely on @nteract namespace for built in transpiling
issue_body:
Following a [comment on the lerna repo](https://github.com/lerna/lerna/issues/487#issuecomment-302341620) we could have a setup that just lets babel do its thing instead of our current pseudo detection from webpack, etc.:

> 1. Set `resolve.symlinks` to `false`. This is needed for the next step.
> 2. Add /@nteract/ regex to the include array of the rule that uses babel-loader.
>   * Since we turned off symlinks resolving in the previous point, other packages from the monorepo are going to resolve to `node_modules/@nteract/<package-name>`, not ../<package-name>.
>   * This was suggested in next.js issue, so I just went to see how they implemented it and did something similar.
> 3. Change node_modules in `resolve.modules` to `path.resolve(__dirname, 'node_modules')`.
>   * Otherwise webpack 2 tried to require dependencies from the node_modules of local linked packages. But that's probably because I use `file:../bar` for specifying them in package.json, as I don't publish them anywhere.
> 4. Set up Jest to transpile files under the @foo scope.
>   * This means adding `"transformIgnorePatterns": ["<rootDir>/node_modules/(?!@nteract)"]` to Jest's configuration. facebook/jest#2550 (comment)
So now exported React components/Flow types can be transpiled by babel!

Not sure if that's wise or if our current setup is the best. I'm just a little burnt out by not knowing if my build is an accurate representation while I'm live editing components.

issue_title: Improve reading notebooks from url that need accept json header
issue_body:
Our notebooks are being stored in an [nbgallery](https://github.com/nbgallery/nbgallery) instance.  A generic `requests.get(notebook_url)` returns html.  Papermill's `execute.execute_notebook(notebook_url)` / `iowr.HttpHandler.read` do not offer a way to set a `{'Accept' : 'application/json'}` header in the request.

When we make a request on our own using accept json headers, we can write that notebook to file (`tempfile` or otherwise) and use Papermill as is.  It would be convenient to also be able to pass in a file-like object (`io.StringIO(resp.text)`), json (`resp.text`), and/or dictionary (`resp.json()`) of the downloaded notebook file rather than needing to write to file.

Thanks.

Mock/MagicMock calls are recorded in order, we may be able to improve the configuration process with a really nice syntax by recording all calls to mock objects then replaying them on real configurator objects once the config parsing is finished.

issue_title: Options method uses wrong signature in calling `super()`
issue_body:
`DjangoTestApp.options` calls its `super` method with `(url, params, headers, extra_environ, status, **kwargs)`, but the actual signature of `TestApp.options` in webtest is `def options(self, url, headers=None, extra_environ=None, status=None, expect_errors=False, xhr=False):` -- note that there is no `params` in the signature.

This can cause a variety of problems when calling `options`, since due to the misalignment of the arguments none of `headers`, `extra_environ`, or `status` are passed correctly.

While fixing this, it may be reasonable to switch to using keyword arguments for all these `super` calls, to catch positional mismatch problems more quickly in the future.

issue_title: Make LCG Tools fail a lot more gracefully on startup for new users
issue_body: There are way too many warnings on startup from the LCG subsystem. We can't have this turned off by default as that will break a lot of current usage but having it fail much more gracefully and less verbosely seems like a good middle ground before we get the credentials stuff in.

I've been thinking about the overall requirements for storing sequences of `GangaObject`s and I've mostly narrowed it down to the following few requirements:

1. In the GPI, proxy-wrap objects as they are extracted from lists and strip proxies as they are added.
2. If the sequence is a schema attribute value:
  - set parent correctly when objects are added to a list and when a list itself is re-parented.
  - check `_readonly` (for locking submitted jobs).
  - set the dirty flag correctly.

If a sequence is not set as a value of a schema attribute then it has no parent, no need to track dirty and no need to track if it's read-only and thus special treatment is not needed (`list` is sufficient).

Currently `GangaList` is designed to provide these features but I feel it has some design flaws which make it a little tricky to use. The main problem I see with it is that it was designed as a `GangaObject` so that it could make sue of the automatic proxy-generation but in the process a lot of code has needed to be added across the project to account for this. For example, it makes it difficult to work out the module dependency tree since `GangaObject` relies on `GangaList` (in getters and setters) but `GangaList` *is a* `GangaObject`.

_As an aside before I continue, I've come to appreciate the distinction between `Node` and `GangaObject`. The former is for managing the hierarchy of parents (and serialisation) while the latter is for auto-proxying and schema-having._

My plan is a simpler replacement for `GangaList` which breaks it into two distinct parts:

- `List` is a subclass of `list` and `Node` which records the parent of all its elements and allows any new elements added to be parented correctly (point 2 above). It does not have a `_schema`. It should be used as an implementation detail of sequence schema attributes.
- `ProxyList` is a GPI-layer wrapper of this which does the proxy-wrapping of its elements (point 1 above).

I have an implementation of the above written which is about 100 lines of code. I believe that is is already in a state to be able to replace `GangaList` and would allow a lot of other code across the project to be simplified. For example it would make a lot of sense to set `SubJobXMLList` to be a subclass of `List`.

This should cover all the use cases discussed in #184 except I have not yet written any of the code to deal specifically with copying `GangaObject`s or assigning via the schema.

Thoughts?

Hi,

I have some concerns on the evaluations used throughout this code repository.

PPO/TRPO

~~The evaluation uses the same trajectories as for training. However, you have a horizon which could be a partial trajectory. However, if you do evaluation on these partial trajectories, you may be skewing your results for the worse. For example, say we have a batch size of 2048 (as is the default with mujoco). And we have two trajectories of 1000 timesteps and then a partial trajectory of 48 timesteps, it looks like our average returns will be much lower than they actually are since we're using the partial trajectory in our evaluation.~~

[EDIT] Nvm, I realized I missed something, it's a bit confusing, but returns only get added here, but partial trajectories can be returned but are only used for the training and not considered in evaluation. https://github.com/openai/baselines/blob/master/baselines/pposgd/pposgd_simple.py#L56-L58

However, that still leaves the question of evaluations are based on an unspecified number of trajectories and then are mixed together with trajectories from past policies since it's a running average of the past 100 trajectories. This is unlike the original TRPO evaluation code it seems, but i guess this is up for debate.

DDPG @matthiasplappert 

First, the algorithm seems a bit off, you collect 100 time steps, then train for 40 timesteps and then evaluate for another 100 and then loop this 20 times. This has the feel of n-step returns without making use of n-step returns. As I understand it originally DDPG should take a small number of steps (ideally one) and use the replay buffer. Second, you're actually evaluating across at least 10 different policies it seems for one trajectory.

https://github.com/openai/baselines/blob/master/baselines/ddpg/training.py#L122-L139

This seems very off. I think the proper thing would be to move the evaluation loop outside of the training loop and do N proper full trajectories with a single policy per epoch. And again, here you may not be evaluating the full trajectory as in PPO.

Maybe I'm misunderstanding the codebase due to the MPI details, but it seems like overall the evaluations done are not proper and can either beneficially or detrimentally change results (even with the 100 average return running mean). Please let me know if I'm incorrect in my assumptions!

issue_title: Excess right-padding when displaying expanded nodes in graph view
issue_body:
When displaying expanded nodes in a Tensorboard graph, a large amount of empty space is generated on the right side of node.  The name is centered in the expanded node, but sub-nodes are positioned off to the left, suggesting a miscalculation of the space required to display the sub-nodes without clipping or excess right-padding (possibly to allocate space for something that isn't required?).  

For operations connected in series, the excess space is about 2/3 of the total space, and the issue becomes more pronounced/significant the more nested layers are expanded.  Issue occurs across both Chrome and Firefox.

Simple graph consisting of a single conv2d node.
![simple_graph](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11787026/37557409-7fcf163e-29da-11e8-9b51-bdfa1fd1fb1c.jpg)

The highest-level node of a more complex graph with multiple branches in parallel.
![high_level](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11787026/37557451-3dc11b6a-29db-11e8-95cc-826625ebdd96.jpg)

The same graph as above when the branches are expanded.  Most of the display space is wasted on excess padding as the issue occurs with each expanded node.  Note that the excess space used is the same between the top-level node and the lower-level nodes, implying a constant excess rather than one proportional to the size of rest of the node.
![expanded](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11787026/37557456-428e60e4-29db-11e8-90b4-d430d25d2ff1.jpg)


Setup details:
 - TensorBoard/TensorFlow version  = 1.6.0
 - OS Platform and version  =   Windows 10
 - Python version  =  3.6.4
 - Browser type and version =  Chrome 64.0.3282.186/Firefox 59.0

issue_title: Remote mode fails to download output
issue_body:
### Expected behavior
After a pipeline completes successfully in remote mode, the results are downloaded to a zip file.

### Observed behavior
Artman down not attempt to download result

### Possible cause
[A recent commit](https://github.com/googleapis/artman/commit/05d74055a85d6295887a76709da17dc648e019c8#diff-41ef7726e048e1a4cd48228eeada3686R48) changed the names of the tasks, but the [conditional that triggers the download](https://github.com/googleapis/artman/blob/master/execute_pipeline.py#L69) still expects the old name. Since there is no task by the old name, the download is never triggered.

issue_title: Certbot-auto error due to sh/bash confusion?
issue_body:
## My operating system is (include version):
SunOS 5.11

## I installed Certbot with (certbot-auto, OS package manager, pip, etc):
certbot-auto

## I ran this command and it produced this output:
```
$ ./certbot-auto --help
./certbot-auto: OLD_VENV_PATH=~/.local/share/letsencrypt: is not an identifier

```
## Certbot's behavior differed from what I expected because:
The certbot-auto script errored on [line 29](https://github.com/certbot/certbot/blob/f510f4bddf4da1411c1966a9cbed2b7e630cba30/certbot-auto#L29).

## Possible explanation

Looks like the script runs in sh while it would actually require bash. See this Stackoverflow post for more info: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/50029500/launching-certbot-auto-fails-on-old-venv-path

Ubuntu 16.04
letsencrypt 0.4.1, installed as per instructions at https://certbot.eff.org/#ubuntuxenial-apache

This is likely related to #3663.

When setting up the auto-renew cron job as per instructions at https://certbot.eff.org/docs/using.html#renewing-certificates, I used the example code for auto-renewing certs, tweaked for our installation:

`letsencrypt renew --agree-tos --email someone@example.com --post-hook "service apache2 restart"`

However, when this runs, I get an error in the logs:

```usage:
  letsencrypt [SUBCOMMAND] [options] [-d domain] [-d domain] ...

The Let's Encrypt agent can obtain and install HTTPS/TLS/SSL certificates.  By
default, it will attempt to use a webserver both for obtaining and installing
the cert. Major SUBCOMMANDS are:

  (default) run        Obtain & install a cert in your current webserver
  certonly             Obtain cert, but do not install it (aka "auth")
  install              Install a previously obtained cert in a server
  renew                Renew previously obtained certs that are near expiry
  revoke               Revoke a previously obtained certificate
  rollback             Rollback server configuration changes made during install
  config_changes       Show changes made to server config during installation
  plugins              Display information about installed plugins
letsencrypt: error: unrecognized arguments: --post-hook service apache2 restart
```

It would be good to update the install instructions so that a user doesn't download the letsencrypt package from the Ubuntu repo, as it does not support all of the listed command flags, which becomes frustrating down the road.  IMHO it should not be the recommended install solution, or a note should be made that it is not kept up-to-date with the upstream package, and alternate install instructions should be included for those that want the latest version.

issue_title: Introduce network state for partner nodes
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

When Raiden is started we must not assume that all partner nodes are reachable

## Solution

All partner nodes must be set to the `unknown` state at startup and the health check should be kicked off at the node startup, once the Pong message is received the node state must be modified from `unknown` to `reachable` if a `max_unresponsive_time` timeout happens it must be modified to `unreachable`.

## Tasklist

- [x] ~Add the state for the nodes in the ChannelGraph, the default value is `unknown`~
- [x] Once the first Pong message is received change the node state to `reachable`
- [x] If a `max_unresponsive_time` timeout happened change the node state to `unreachable`
- [x] Add a synchronization `wait_token_network` with this semantics:
  - Block until one of the nodes moves to the `reachable` state or all nodes are at the `unreachable`.

issue_title: Matrix must process received messages synchronously when being started
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Raiden's matrix transport at the moment syncs and handles invite events. However, with #2086, we should ensure that all events received are processed synchronously in the matrix `start` before returning. This makes sure that the API server will start iff Raiden is in sync and has processed all events received from the blockchain and transport.

This is required as we need to make sure that the API doesn't get called while the matrix is still syncing messages since in this case, the message queue will contain messages with the wrong order.

According to my discussion with @andrevmatos, this issue depends on #2081. That will help identify a checkpoint with which we can identify processed vs pending messages and only process pending messages that don't have a read receipt.

issue_title: Use getattr rather than __dict__ in Batch (adds support for __slots__ in Example subclasses)
issue_body:
This is a proposal to change [one line of code](https://github.com/pytorch/text/blob/c839a7934930819be7e240ea972e4d600966afdc/torchtext/data/batch.py#L27) in Batch.py

I suggest `[x.__dict__[name] for x in data]` should become `[getattr(x, name) for x in data]`

A major advantage to doing this is compatibility with __slots__. A class that is going to be instantiated for every data point is an ideal use-case for __slots__, which reduces per-instance memory overhead. It makes sense for specific projects to subclass Example using __slots__ with the known fields of the project. If you do, the instances will have empty __dicts__ but the slots can be accessed via getattr.

I don't _think_ this change would break anything...

issue_title: Add Support for Send OS Command' operation
Gesture to copy configuration from one stack to another
issue_body:
This is a quality of life improvement.

Pulumi makes it very easy to spin up new stacks, which is great.  However, when you create a new stack, you must configure it "from scratch".  The tempting thing to do is to copy an existing `Pulumi.<stack-name>.yaml` file and make the minor edits.  After all, it's just a file right?

Unfortunately, if you are using any encrypted configuration values, things quickly go off the rails.  Since configuration values are encrypted with a per stack key, simply copying the file will not do the right thing.  So, at a minimum, you need to reset all of the secret configuration values, which is a chore.

We should consider a gesture like:

`pulumi config copy [source-stack-name] [destination-stack-name]` which copies all of the configuration values from the source stack to the destination stack.  We could, choose to further augment this with something like a  `--value` flag which would let you specify one or more values to copy.

In the case where we would overwrite values in the destination stack, we could  issue a warning and prompt the user to answer yes or no.

issue_title: Unify resource management between tasks and actors.
issue_body:
There are some inconsistencies in the way we are managing resources (CPUs and GPUs).

- Right the local scheduler ignores the dynamic resource capacity of the node when scheduling actor tasks.
- Tasks that use GPUs don't have access to `ray.get_gpu_ids()`.
- When we reserve GPUs for actors and GPUs for tasks, these things are completely disjoint from one another. For example, GPUs are reserved for tasks by local schedulers and GPUs are reserved for actors by Redis. They should both be done by the local scheduler.

issue_title: Non unicode escaped API breaks the plugin
issue_body:
I have a problem on Streamlink 0.0.2 with Twitch plugin. Luckily I figured out what's going on and fixed my own copy of code. So I would like to share my fix though I am not sure with my decision.

My question is:
> Is it safe to assume that JSON APIs other than Twitch, use particular text encoding?


Here is some error log.
```
> streamlink.exe --player <some my player> --twitch-oauth-token <redacted> https://twitch.tv/<readacted> source
[cli][info] Found matching plugin twitch for URL https://twitch.tv/<readacted>
[plugin.twitch][info] Attempting to authenticate using OAuth token
error: 'cp949' codec can't encode character '\u011b' in position 48: illegal multibyte sequence
```

Little backgrounds.

1. Twitch API does not escape 'non-ascii' characters.
2. Twitch API does not state `charset` in the `Content-type` header. Only states MIME type `application/json`.
3. I am running Korean version of Windows 10, and the plugin tries to decode response body with OS default encoding(presumably) which is CP949 in my case.

Let me follow the code flow.

1. Twitch plugin tries to authenticate with my OAuth token, in https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/blob/0.0.2/src/streamlink/plugins/twitch.py#L264
2. ...which is `user` method in helper class, in https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/blob/0.0.2/src/streamlink/plugins/twitch.py#L208
3. ...which requests API with GET method, in https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/blob/0.0.2/src/streamlink/plugins/twitch.py#L167
4. I know Twitch API returns in JSON format, so going https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/blob/0.0.2/src/streamlink/plugins/twitch.py#L182
5. ...which tries to parse response body text in JSON, implicitly tries to decode response body from bytes to str, in https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/blob/0.0.2/src/streamlink/plugin/api/http_session.py#L70

I could simply edit `http_session.py` and fix it by stating 'known' charset before accessing `text` property, something like:
```
+        if res.encoding is None:
+            res.encoding = 'utf8'
         return parse_json(res.text, *args, **kwargs)
```

issue_title: Include functionality to consume CopHub CSV catalogues?
issue_body:
As of 2017-09-04 the Copernicus Open Access Hub has published monthly CSV files containing all products published during that time and available through the CopHub ([source](https://scihub.copernicus.eu/userguide/6CatalogueViewInCSV)).

Is this something that should be consumeable by `sentinelsat` in addition to the search of the DHuS?

Currently they are structured as 1 CSV file per month containing the following metadata:

- ID | The Universally Unique Identifier (UUID) of the product 
- Name | The product filename 
- ContentLength | The size in bytes of the .ZIP file 
- IngestionDate | The date and time of the ingestion of the product in the data hub system 
- ContentDate:Start* | The sensing start date and time of the product 
- ContentDate:End* | The sensing stop date and time of the product 
- Checksum:Algorithm | The hash function name used by the Data Hub system
- Checksum:Value | Hash value 

Using these we might be able to query large amounts of data faster if the search area is exceptionally large (continents and larger). On the other hand, no geographic relation of the datasets is stored.

Currently I lean towards not including functionality to use these CSV datasets as I can see no advantage to the usage of `sentinelsat`. I would like to have a discussion about this, maybe it is useful for people ingesting larger amounts of data (such as @valgur ?).

issue_title: Raising an exception on scope mismatch is too strict
issue_body:
The following use case described Office Graph API authentication flow. MS uses the `offline_access` scope as a flag that a refresh token should be provided with the access token: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/onedrive/developer/rest-api/getting-started/graph-oauth#authentication-scopes

When authenticating though, it does not return that particular scope in the response, resulting in an exception:
```python
scopes = "offline_access Files.ReadWrite.All User.Read Group.ReadWrite.All Sites.ReadWrite.All Directory.ReadWrite.All Mail.Read User.ReadWrite.All"

oauth = OAuth2Session(client_id, redirect_uri=redirect_url, scope=scopes)
authorization_url, state = oauth.authorization_url(
    url=auth_url,
    response_mode='query',
    state='12345'
)
webbrowser.open_new(authorization_url)

auth_response = input('Response url')

token = oauth.fetch_token(
    token_url,
    authorization_response=auth_response,
    client_secret=client_secret,
)
```
```text
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/orcarmi/PycharmProjects/shield/oauth_porc.py", line 28, in <module>
    client_secret=client_secret,
  File "/Users/orcarmi/PycharmProjects/shield/venv/lib/python3.6/site-packages/requests_oauthlib/oauth2_session.py", line 244, in fetch_token
    self._client.parse_request_body_response(r.text, scope=self.scope)
  File "/Users/orcarmi/PycharmProjects/shield/venv/lib/python3.6/site-packages/oauthlib/oauth2/rfc6749/clients/base.py", line 411, in parse_request_body_response
    self.token = parse_token_response(body, scope=scope)
  File "/Users/orcarmi/PycharmProjects/shield/venv/lib/python3.6/site-packages/oauthlib/oauth2/rfc6749/parameters.py", line 379, in parse_token_response
    validate_token_parameters(params)
  File "/Users/orcarmi/PycharmProjects/shield/venv/lib/python3.6/site-packages/oauthlib/oauth2/rfc6749/parameters.py", line 409, in validate_token_parameters
    raise w
Warning: Scope has changed from "Directory.ReadWrite.All offline_access Mail.Read User.ReadWrite.All User.Read Files.ReadWrite.All Group.ReadWrite.All Sites.ReadWrite.All" to "Directory.ReadWrite.All Mail.Read User.ReadWrite.All User.Read Files.ReadWrite.All Group.ReadWrite.All Sites.ReadWrite.All".
```
According to the [RFC](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749#section-3.3), this is valid, since they return the updated `scope` in the response: 
>The authorization server MAY fully or partially ignore the scope
   requested by the client, based on the authorization server policy or
   the resource owner's instructions.  If the issued access token scope
   is different from the one requested by the client, the authorization
   server MUST include the "scope" response parameter to inform the
   client of the actual scope granted

The workaround for this is setting the `OAUTHLIB_RELAX_TOKEN_SCOPE` envar.

Perhaps an exception should be raised only if the response does not contain the `scope` parameter at all, which will violate the RFC.

I have 1x webserver, 1x json_rpc_server and 1x xml_rpc_server. I also have 3 workers. (everything runs on the same box)

When the worker's startup, they each have the same list of 3 possible sockets: 
         ["tcp://127.0.0.1:9005","tcp://127.0.0.1:9006","tcp://127.0.0.1:9007"]

they each iterate through the list possible addresses until zmq does not throw an exception (see https://github.com/rbucker881/spotify_test/blob/master/src/spotify_test/echo.py)

Once the worker successfully binds it performs a blocking read.


On the web server side... each web server connects to the three addresses. When a transaction arrives, the web server performs a send(). The send() round robins nicely.


However, if one of the echo workers is killed, then the send() blocks when the round robin finally gets back to that server.


if, however, the web server binds and the worker connects, then client is able to round robin.


The complete example code is here: https://github.com/rbucker881/spotify_test

issue_title: pyzmq hard locks Windows 7 with fast socket open/close
issue_body:
Found this out the hard way with some buggy code that used to work "fine" on a Windows XP box.

This is probably a libzmq issue, and not specifically a pyzmq issue, but I have never written any C which uses libzmq, so am not sure how to recreate it. I thought I would post it here for anyone who encounters the issue.

While the code below is not ideal, (like I said, it was found in something buggy), it seems it shouldn't be able to hard lock the machine. I have been able to lock up the client with a sleep of one to five seconds between sockets occasionally as well, though inconsistently.

## Example Server
```python
import zmq

def run():
    ctx = zmq.Context()

    sock = ctx.socket(zmq.REP)
    sock.bind('tcp://127.0.0.1:5555')

    print "Waiting..."
    while True:
       msg = sock.recv()
       sock.send('10-4')
       print msg

if __name__ == '__main__':
    run()
```

## Example Client
```python
import zmq

def connect_and_send(ctx, msg):
    sock = ctx.socket(zmq.REQ)
    sock.connect('tcp://127.0.0.1:5555')
    sock.send(msg)
    sock.recv()
    sock.close()

def run():
    ctx = zmq.Context()

    count = 1
    while True:
        connect_and_send(ctx, "Msg %s" % count)
        count += 1

if __name__ == '__main__':
    run()
```
